name: happy-path-variant-b
task: Set up Row Level Security on a multi-tenant SaaS application using Supabase. We have a `projects` table and a `tasks` table. Each user should only see projects they own and tasks belonging to those projects. Write the RLS policies.
assertions: Response includes `ALTER TABLE projects ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY` and `ALTER TABLE tasks ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY`
Response creates RLS policies using `auth.uid()` to match the current user against an owner/user column
Response creates separate policies for SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, and/or DELETE operations (or uses `FOR ALL` with a `USING` clause), not a single blanket grant
Response includes `FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY` or mentions that table owners bypass RLS without it
Response uses `USING` clause for read filtering and `WITH CHECK` clause for write validation in the policy definitions
